mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, which reduces corporate profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances when the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over three months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand and consumer confidence, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when both series exceed their 12-month moving averages, with a hit rate below 45% over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which supports near-term corporate earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Weekly forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero during periods of industrial production acceleration, or the signal predictive power vanishes when inflation exceeds 4%.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with persistently low policy rates creates negative real yields that trigger market expectations of abrupt monetary tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as discount rates rise.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a positive or neutral return signal when CPI exceeds 4.0% and policy rates remain below 0.5%, with a Rank IC > 0.05 contradicting the negative prediction.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth falling below 1.0 percent indicates severe economic contraction and weakening business activity, which directly reduces equity earnings forecasts and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in 70% of quarters where GDP growth falls below 1.0%, invalidating the contraction signal.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding labor demand and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and corporate profitability, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Monthly payroll growth shows no statistically significant positive correlation with quarterly equity returns over a 10-year rolling window, or the signal Sharpe ratio falls below 0.5.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production and retail sales demonstrates balanced supply-demand expansion without inventory gluts, which sustains corporate margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative returns when both series grow simultaneously, with a hit rate below 40% across multiple economic cycles.
